accurate.why: The completed portion (PL 119-3, S.J. Res. 11, approved March 14, 2025, disapproves a federal regulation) is accurate and consistent with the bill text.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated or false claims appear in the truncated text; all stated facts match the bill.

states_purpose.why: It states the primary purpose is to nullify a federal rule via a joint resolution of disapproval, though the sentence cuts off.

changes_to_existing_law.why: The summary cuts off before identifying the BOEM Marine Archaeological Resources rule or the Congressional Review Act mechanism, so it fails to describe the legal effect on existing regulation.

exceptions_conditions.why: The disapproval resolution contains no material exceptions or carve-outs to summarize.

effective_dates.why: The summary correctly states the resolution was approved March 14, 2025.

major_provisions_covered.why: The summary is truncated before naming the rule being nullified or its operative effect (no force or effect), missing the bill's essential content.

objective_tone.why: The text is neutral and contains no advocacy or editorializing.

coherent.why: The summary ends mid-sentence ('the rule submitted by the'), leaving it incomplete and not reading as finished prose.

concise.why: The text contains no padding or repetition.

correct_entities.why: Public Law 119-3 and Senate Joint Resolution 11 are correctly identified.

correct_figures.why: The only figures/dates stated—PL 119-3 and March 14, 2025—are correct.
